terms.definition: Instance-based supervised learner that classifies or regresses from the k training examples closest to a query point by a distance metric. Canonical algorithm treatment in this topic.
Learning paradigm that defers generalization to query time by storing training data instead of fitting a compact model during training. k-NN is the classic lazy learner.
Methods that predict by comparing new inputs to stored training instances (exemplars) rather than explicit global parameters. k-NN is the standard example.
k-NN classification rule: assign the class label that appears most frequently among the k nearest neighbors; ties need a defined resolution.
k-NN regression rule: predict the mean (or weighted mean) of target values y among the k nearest training points.
Count of neighbors consulted for each prediction; controls smoothness of the induced decision boundary and the bias–variance tradeoff.
